primary_reasons: Dynamic representation switching is interesting but not entirely novel.
Similar ideas exist in low-rank factorization and quantization.
Implementation lacks clarity on specific performance metrics.
Falsifiability could be improved with clearer expected outcomes.
revision_instructions: To enhance novelty, consider integrating a unique mechanism for layer representation beyond existing quantization and low-rank techniques. Explore alternative adaptive strategies that do not rely solely on gradient norms. Additionally, clarify the expected performance metrics and how they will be measured during implementation.
